--- SOURCE 1: STEM for Everyone: FIRST Initiatives and Grants ---
URL: https://www.firstinspires.org/stem-for-everyone/initiatives-grants

SUMMARY:
FIRST® is actively engaged in developing strategies, collaborations, grants, and initiatives that ensure opportunities for any young person who participates in FIRST programs. FIRST launched its STEM Community Innovation Grants program in 2016 to provide students across the globe with hands-on learning and creative problem-solving opportunities. To enhance the contributions of girls, as well as those of other participant youth in STEM, we must support all students throughout their participation in FIRST programs and experiences on teams. As a youth-serving organization, our commitment to STEM for Everyone is in service of helping all young people find belonging and thrive as part of our robotics teams and community.



--------------------------------------------------------------------------------


--- SOURCE 2: FIRST | For Inspiration and Recognition of Science and ... ---
URL: https://www.firstinspires.org/

SUMMARY:
Founded in 1989 by inventor Dean Kamen, _FIRST_® is a global nonprofit organization that prepares young people for the future through a suite of life-changing youth robotics programs that build skills, confidence, and resilience. Participants gain real-world problem-solving experiences through a guided, global robotics program using LEGO® technology, helping today’s students and teachers build a better future together. Guided by mentors and sponsors, students learn engineering and problem-solving skills as they compete for awards, create a team identity, raise funds, hone teamwork skills, and advance appreciation for STEM in their community. #### Press Release: 50,000+ Celebrate STEM Skills & Innovation as International Youth Robotics Teams Compete at _FIRST_® Championship in Houston

A GLOBAL ROBOTICS COMMUNITY PREPARING YOUNG PEOPLE FOR THE FUTURE

We Are the World's Leading Youth-Serving Nonprofit Advancing STEM Education
---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Founded in 1989 by inventor Dean Kamen, _FIRST_® is a global nonprofit organization that prepares young people for the future through a suite of life-changing youth robotics programs that build skills, confidence, and resilience.

[Read About Our Mission, Purpose & Values](https://www.firstinspires.org/about)

![Image 2](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/i-am-first-vid-image2.png)

_FIRST_ Helps Kids "Get Anywhere"
---------------------------------

The breadth of career opportunities that an education in STEM provides is wider than ever. Today’s students are inspired by _FIRST_ to build futures tailored to their unique talents and interests.

[Learn More](https://info.firstinspires.org/getanywhere?utm_source=first-inspires&utm_medium=promo&utm_campaign=get-anywhere-025)

![Image 3](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/default/files/2024-2025-FIRST/get-anywhere-homepage-2.jpg)

Inspiring Generations of Global Citizens
----------------------------------------

![Image 4](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/global-icon.svg)

**3.2 million youth** participants since 1989

![Image 5](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/teamwork-icon.svg)

_FIRST_ Robotics teams are active in **110+ countries.**

![Image 6](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/grad-icon.svg)

83% of **_FIRST_ alumni declare majors in STEM**by their fourth year of college

![Image 7](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/flag-icon.svg)

**Over 96% of _FIRST_ students persevere**despite challenges or barriers as a result of program participation

[Find More Facts](https://www.firstinspires.org/about/impact)

A Suite of Hands-On STEM Programs for Every Age
-----------------------------------------------

Project-based, hands-on _FIRST_ programs introduce students to engineering and coding in engaging, inclusive, and creative learning environments, where students work collaboratively to solve themed robotics challenges.

Because our programs are _More Than Robots_®, participants are guided by _FIRST_ Core Values to build a better future.

Children can join any of our three programs based on age or grade level. Ages may vary by region.

![Image 15](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/fll-white.png)

GRADES 

PreK-8

GRADES PreK-8

AGES 4-16

_FIRST_® LEGO® League introduces STEM to children through fun, exciting hands-on learning. Participants gain real-world problem-solving experiences through a guided, global robotics program using LEGO® technology, helping today’s students and teachers build a better future together. _FIRST_ LEGO League’s three divisions inspire youth to experiment and grow their critical thinking, coding, and design skills and showcase what they learned.

![Image 16](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/fll-play-button.svg)

_FIRST_ LEGO League is...

[Learn More About **_FIRST_ LEGO League**](https://www.firstinspires.org/robotics/fll)

![Image 17](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/ftc-white.png)

GRADES 

7-12

GRADES 7-12

AGES 12-18

_FIRST_® Tech Challenge students work together with their mentors to design and build robots to compete in a dynamic and exciting challenge released every September. Teams program classroom-scale robots to follow autonomous commands before student drivers take control in two-on-two matches. On and off the field, students develop STEM skills, engage in community outreach, practice engineering innovation, and build confidence to help them succeed.

![Image 18](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/ftc-play-button.svg)

_FIRST_ Tech Challenge is...

[Learn More About **_FIRST_ Tech Challenge**](https://www.firstinspires.org/robotics/ftc)

![Image 19](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/frc-white.png)

GRADES 

9-12

GRADES 9-12

AGES 14-18

Each January brings a new challenge for _FIRST_® Robotics Competition teams. Starting with a Kit of Parts, they design, program, and build industrial-sized robots to play an action-packed game on a themed field as part of a three-team alliance. Guided by mentors and sponsors, students learn engineering and problem-solving skills as they compete for awards, create a team identity, raise funds, hone teamwork skills, and advance appreciation for STEM in their community.

![Image 20](https://www.firstinspires.org/sites/all/themes/first/assets/images/2020/frc-play-button.svg)

_FIRST_ Robotics Competition is...

[Learn More About **_FIRST_ Robotics Competition**](https://www.firstinspires.org/robotics/frc)

--- SOURCE 3: Guide to Education Grants: How to Get Funding for Your ... ---
URL: https://kidsparkeducation.org/blog/guide-to-education-grants-how-to-get-funding-for-your-stem-program

SUMMARY:
An overview of education grants and how they can help fund a STEM program; How to find relevant grant opportunities for your STEM program goals

FULL CONTENT:
![Kid Spark Education](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hubfs/Logo/KiDSpark-Education-Logo-light.png)
![Kid Spark Education](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hubfs/Logo/KiDSpark-Education-Logo-dark.png)

# Guide to Education Grants: How to Get Funding for Your STEM Program

![STEM grants provide a unique opportunity to bring STEM education to your school. Learn how to get grant funding in this step-by-step guide.](https://5375473.fs1.hubspotusercontent-na1.net/hub/5375473/hubfs/Guide%20to%20Education%20Grants_%20How%20to%20Get%20Funding%20for%20Your%20STEM%20Program.jpg?width=1200&height=650&name=Guide%20to%20Education%20Grants_%20How%20to%20Get%20Funding%20for%20Your%20STEM%20Program.jpg)

### A comprehensive guide to education grants for school and district administrators. Learn about the different types of grants available, how to find grants you qualify for, and tips for writing a winning grant proposal.

![Guide to Education Grants_ How to Get Funding for Your STEM Program](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/Guide%20to%20Education%20Grants_%20How%20to%20Get%20Funding%20for%20Your%20STEM%20Program.jpg?width=770&height=400&name=Guide%20to%20Education%20Grants_%20How%20to%20Get%20Funding%20for%20Your%20STEM%20Program.jpg)

![Guide to Education Grants_ How to Get Funding for Your STEM Program](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/Guide%20to%20Education%20Grants_%20How%20to%20Get%20Funding%20for%20Your%20STEM%20Program.jpg?width=770&height=400&name=Guide%20to%20Education%20Grants_%20How%20to%20Get%20Funding%20for%20Your%20STEM%20Program.jpg)

With school and district budgets stretched thin in recent years, education grants have been vital to funding new and innovative STEM programs in schools and districts across the country. Government, private, and corporate education grants can help close budget gaps, allowing schools in need to provide comprehensive STEM curricula to their students In this comprehensive guide, we’ll cover the different types of education grants available and how they can help fund your vision for a rewarding STEM learning experience for your students. Whether your goal is to close a budget gap to maintain a current STEM program or to launch an entirely new curriculum, grants can help turn these ideas into reality.

In this article, you’ll find:

Are you ready to get your dream STEM program funded? Through strategic planning, targeted proposals, and effective grant management, the resources you need can be within your reach. This guide to STEM education grants is designed to help equip you with the key knowledge and practical steps to successfully secure funding for STEM education at your school or district. So let’s get started!

![Funding Your New STEM Curriculum](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/Funding%20Your%20New%20STEM%20Curriculum.jpg?width=770&height=400&name=Funding%20Your%20New%20STEM%20Curriculum.jpg)

![Funding Your New STEM Curriculum](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/Funding%20Your%20New%20STEM%20Curriculum.jpg?width=770&height=400&name=Funding%20Your%20New%20STEM%20Curriculum.jpg)

## Everything You Need to Know About STEM Education Grants

Education grants from government agencies, foundations, nonprofit organizations, corporations, and private donors can provide crucial funding for K-12 schools looking to implement new STEM curricula and programming.

Below are the most common questions administrators have when starting to explore the world of education grants for STEM.

**What is an education grant?**

An education grant is a sum of money given by a government, company, foundation, or organization to support education programs and initiatives. Unlike loans, education grants do not need to be repaid by the recipient, but they do have terms that need to be followed, such as eligibility criteria, reporting certain metrics and results, fund matching, or itemized qualified expenses.

**What type of STEM programs and activities can be funded by an education grant?**

Education grants can fund a wide range of STEM-related programs and initiatives, such as:

Ultimately, each STEM education grant will outline what the funds can be used for and how to maintain compliance to ensure that your school or district doesn’t need to pay back any grant funds because of misallocation.

**Who is eligible to apply for STEM education grants?**

Any educational institution can receive a STEM education grant, though some grants might specify the type of institution or grade level they are looking for. Without any restrictions, however, K-12 schools and school districts, colleges, universities, nonprofit organizations, libraries, and museums can all be recipients of educational grants in STEM.

**When do you need to apply for education grants?**

Applications for STEM education grants typically open in yearly, biannual, or quarterly cycles. Any new proposals will be reviewed based on these individual schedules, so it’s important to keep track of each grant’s application deadline.

**Is there an application fee for applying for education grants?**

Most government and nonprofit education grants do not charge an application fee. However, a very small number of corporate grants do require a small application fee.

**How much funding do STEM education grants provide?**

Award amounts for education grants vary greatly. Anywhere from $1,000 to multi-million dollar grants are available depending on the project size, the grantor, and the individual grant terms.

**Can you fund a STEM program with more than one education grant?**

Yes, it is absolutely possible and common to fund a STEM program through multiple monetary resources and education grants. Securing money from more than one grant source allows schools and districts to maximize their funding.

**How competitive is it to get an education grant for STEM?**

STEM education grants have seen a rise in application volume, so having a standout proposal is key to finding funding success.

![How to Fund a STEM Program with the Help of Education Grants (A Step-by-Step Guide)](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/How%20to%20Fund%20a%20STEM%20Program%20with%20the%20Help%20of%20Education%20Grants%20(A%20Step-by-Step%20Guide).jpg?width=770&height=400&name=How%20to%20Fund%20a%20STEM%20Program%20with%20the%20Help%20of%20Education%20Grants%20(A%20Step-by-Step%20Guide).jpg)

![How to Fund a STEM Program with the Help of Education Grants (A Step-by-Step Guide)](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/How%20to%20Fund%20a%20STEM%20Program%20with%20the%20Help%20of%20Education%20Grants%20(A%20Step-by-Step%20Guide).jpg?width=770&height=400&name=How%20to%20Fund%20a%20STEM%20Program%20with%20the%20Help%20of%20Education%20Grants%20(A%20Step-by-Step%20Guide).jpg)

## How to Fund a STEM Program with the Help of Education Grants (A Step-by-Step Guide)

### 1. Define Your STEM Program

Before you start requesting funding from education grants, you need a clear vision of your STEM program. This will help you find the most relevant grant opportunities, craft compelling grant proposals, and maintain compliance with each grant’s requirements.

If you’re not sure where to start with defining your STEM program, check out our blog post on [how to start a STEM program in your school district](https://blog.kidsparkeducation.org/blog/how-to-start-a-stem-program-in-your-school-district).

### 2. Find STEM Education Grants for Your School

Your first stop in finding education grants for your STEM program should be a comprehensive database like those provided by [Grants.gov](https://www.grants.gov/search-grants), the [National Science Foundation](https://new.nsf.gov/funding/opportunities), and the [Department of Education](https://www2.ed.gov/fund/grants-apply.html).

These databases compile STEM-related grant opportunities and provide built-in filters so you can narrow your search to find education grants that your school qualifies for.

You can also find curated lists of private and corporate STEM grant opportunities on sites like [Solid Professor](https://www.solidprofessor.com/blog/a-comprehensive-list-of-grants-for-teachers-find-funding-for-your-stem-curriculum/#grants%20available%20for%20your%20state), [Snohomish STEM](https://snohomishstem.org/stem-grants-educators/), or [Instrumentl](https://www.instrumentl.com/browse-grants/stem-education-grants?filter_by=location).

Continue your search by looking for local and regional education grants from private and corporate sources. Don’t forget that regional STEM funders can come from hidden gems like community foundations and local private donors in your city or state who might offer smaller STEM education grants only available to regional schools, not nationwide applicants.

You might also want to connect with local grant writers in your area, as they may be willing to share upcoming local grant opportunities that circle among school districts. Tapping into these hyper-local education grant pools can provide the STEM funding other schools may overlook, giving you better odds at success.

### 3. Choosing the Right Education Grant for Your STEM Program

Since you’ve clearly defined the STEM program you are aiming to fund, finding relevant STEM grants will be much easier. Keep these key factors in mind when determining if an education grant is a good fit for your STEM program:

### 4. Write a Winning Grant Proposal

When writing your STEM grant proposal, there are a few key tips to keep in mind to make your proposal more competitive and compelling:

**What do grantors look for when evaluating applications for STEM education grants?** In general, funders are looking for a demonstrated need for the funds, the expected outcomes from receiving the funds, and the feasibility of your proposed STEM program. In addition to these, some specialized grant competitions may be more focused on the geographical region, creativity or innovation, specific reporting metrics, or other individual criteria.

**Who should write the grant proposal for a STEM education grant?**

The best person to write the STEM grant proposal depends on the size and resources of your school or district. If you have the resources, a dedicated grant writer may be beneficial. They continuously build relationships with funders and are familiar with the detailed requirements STEM grants require.

If a grant writer is not an option, a school or district administrator, STEM director, teacher, or a cross-functional team with all of the above can help write a compelling STEM grant proposal that tells a story and paints the individual needs of your school or district.

### 5. Maintain Grant Compliance and Properly Manage Your STEM Grant

STEM grants require varied compliance and fiscal responsibility from each grant recipient. This helps keep organizations accountable to their funding sources, standardize grant reporting requirements, and further any internal goals the grantors might have, such as impact quantification or reporting.

Keep a dedicated team and a detailed calendar to oversee the project management of your grant to ensure that every aspect of your STEM grant stays aligned with expectations and within compliance. This might include:

## Funding Your New STEM Curriculum

STEM grants provide a unique opportunity to bring external funding to your school or district. This can make all the difference when it comes to implementing a new STEM curriculum for your students.

With these tips on how to secure a STEM education grant for your school(s), we hope you find the funding you need! You can also learn more about our [STEM Equity Grants Program](https://go.kidsparkeducation.org/grants/stem-equity-grant-programs) to see if you qualify.

## Everything You Need to Know About STEM Education Grants

![Everything You Need to Know About STEM Education Grants](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/Everything%20You%20Need%20to%20Know%20About%20STEM%20Education%20Grants.jpg?width=770&height=400&name=Everything%20You%20Need%20to%20Know%20About%20STEM%20Education%20Grants.jpg)

## Partner with Kid Spark Education for a Comprehensive STEM Program

Kid Spark Education is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization dedicated to making STEM education accessible to *all* students. Since 2015, we’ve brought Kid Spark STEM labs to hundreds of institutions—many using won grant funds to purchase our programs. We would love to do the same for you and provide your schools with the tools you need for STEM success.

Our research-backed STEM curriculum for Pre-K through 8th grade provides an [all-in-one district solution](https://go.kidsparkeducation.org/district-administration) that spans grade levels and builds a solid foundation in STEM fluencies through hands-on lesson plans and activities, cumulative curricula, and NGSS-aligned STEM objectives.

To learn more about our STEM labs and see if they’re the right fit for your school, [contact us today](https://kidsparkeducation.org/contact).

![KSE Footer Graphic Divider Element New](https://kidsparkeducation.org/hs-fs/hubfs/KSE%20Footer%20Graphic%20Divider%20Element%20New.png?width=1640&height=514&name=KSE%20Footer%20Graphic%20Divider%20Element%20New.png)

## We help children learn and love STEM

Kid Spark Education is one of the only organizations in the United States focused on STEM solutions for the youngest learners facing the greatest barriers to STEM education.  
  
Learn more about our **[STEM education solutions](/stem-education-solutions)**, our **[STEM Grants Initiative](/stem-equity-grants-initiative)**, and **[how to partner with us](/partnerships)**.

### **Your One-Stop STEM Grant Resource!**

At STEMfinity, our aim is to promote wider access to STEM education throughout the nation. We recognize that educators require access to diverse funding sources to equip their students with the latest and most effective hands-on STEM enrichment products. Our mission is to support you in this endeavor!

### **Your One-Stop STEM Grant Resource!**

At STEMfinity, our aim is to promote wider access to STEM education throughout the nation. We recognize that educators require access to diverse funding sources to equip their students with the latest and most effective hands-on STEM enrichment products. Our mission is to support you in this endeavor!

To that end, we are developing tools to assist educators seeking funding. This includes a general toolkit, upcoming webinars, and other resources. We also maintain a comprehensive directory of national, state, and regional grants. Bookmark this page and visit often!

We are also launching free grant-seeking and grant-writing assistance to qualified applicants. Please fill out [this questionnaire](https://stemfinity.com/pages/stem-grant-questionnaire) to see if you qualify and for more information.

Grants are awarded based on the availability of funds, geographic representation, inclusion of underrepresented groups in cybersecurity (e.g. girls, racial/ethnic minorities, persons with disabilities), and demonstrated need, with preference for applicants who intend to use CYBER.ORG curriculum materials. --- SOURCE 5: STEM concepts promote exciting future ---
URL: https://www.aep.com/news/stories/view/9885/STEM-concepts-promote-exciting-future-/

SUMMARY:
Grants help robotics teams compete at local, regional and international levels, where they learn teamwork, critical thinking and technical

FULL CONTENT:
![American Electric Power](/logos/aep-logo-red-gray.svg)

# STEM concepts promote exciting future

A solid foundation in science, technology, engineering and math is critical to success in school and future careers.

![](https://aepcdn.com/aepcomimages/img_1730985795647.jpg?size=medium)

For utility professionals, STEM concepts fuel critical thinking skills and help develop innovative solutions to everyday energy practices. Our goal is to make STEM education accessible and engaging, preparing students for the evolving world.

From preschool to high school, educators work to instill a passion for STEM in students.

Through partnerships with schools and local organizations, we promote access to hands-on experience of STEM concepts. One such opportunity is Junior Achievement's 3DE program. Students applied STEM concepts to solve problems and develop an innovative solution for their project. This included coaching and a day at I&M's Northeast Service Center. Additional opportunities just within I&M's service area have been made possible by the Boys and Girls Clubs, the Fort Wayne Urban League, Elkhart Education Foundation and more.

![](https://aepcdn.com/aepcomimages/img_1730985693332.jpg?size=medium)

With support from the American Electric Power Foundation, the [FIRST Robotics](https://www.aep.com/community/education) grants also provides support for STEM education. Grants help robotics teams compete at local, regional and international levels, where they learn teamwork, critical thinking and technical skills.

Meanwhile, the [Teacher Vision](https://www.aep.com/community/education) grant is designed to improve academic achievement with a focus on STEM, electrical safety and the balanced study of energy and the environment.

enriched_points.expanded_content: The FIRST Grants program, initiated in 2016, aims to provide funding for STEM education, enhancing accessibility for underrepresented youth in robotics competitions globally.
Numerous teams have thrived with FIRST Grants, showcasing improved skills, confidence, and participation rates, particularly among girls and underserved communities, fostering a sense of belonging.
Despite successes, challenges include limited funding allocations, competition for grants, and barriers faced by teams in rural or economically disadvantaged areas needing additional support.

Programs highlight the importance of mentorship, community engagement, and targeted outreach, emphasizing that strategic partnerships can significantly enhance program effectiveness and sustainability.
Future initiatives may focus on expanding grant availability, increasing outreach efforts to underserved populations, and developing resources to support teams in maximizing grant utilization.
